audit_items.proof_trace: In `text_open`, `calloc` results are guarded only by `assert()` (L213, L216), which is a no-op in release builds (NDEBUG). If either `calloc` call fails, `data` or `data->buffer` is NULL, and the immediately following struct-member assignment (`data->buffer_length = ...`) or `text_update`'s `*buf++ = ...` is an unconditional NULL dereference. However, all callers of the meter API (slapadd.c, slapmodify.c, back-mdb/tools.c) are offline administrative tools — not the live slapd LDAP request handler — so the stated LDAP external reachability does not hold.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An administrator running `slapadd` or `slapmodify` under memory pressure (or an attacker causing OOM via repeated large LDAP requests to exhaust server memory before an admin operation) could trigger the calloc failure in `text_open`, crashing the admin tool via NULL dereference at `data->buffer_length = default_buffer_length` (L214).
